original: Syracuse is captured by the Muslim sultan of Sicily
Pope John VIII gives blessings to duke Branimir and to Croatian people, considered to be international recognition of the Croatian state
Pope Gregory V crowns his cousin Otto III as Holy Roman Emperor in St Peter's Basilica in Rome
King Henry III gives Utrecht the Groninger currency
Kublai Khan's second invasion of Japan begins with an attack on Tsushima Island but encounters fierce resistance, forcing his troops to withdraw
Earthquake centered on Dover Straits with estimated magnitude of 6.0 causes widespread damage, including to Canterbury Cathedral
Portuguese explorer João Da Nova discovers the uninhabited Saint Helena island in the South Atlantic Ocean
Martha's Vineyard is first sighted by Captain Bartholomew Gosnold
France, England, and the Netherlands sign the Concert of The Hague, outlining a common stance on how the Second Northern War should end
General John Sobieski chosen King of Poland
West Indian Company sells 1/3 of Suriname
Mary Campbell is abducted from her home in Pennsylvania by Lenape during the French and Indian War
Mount Unzen on Japan's Shimabara Peninsula erupts, creating a tsunami and killing about 15,000 people in the country's deadliest volcanic eruption
Curacao Island Council forbids criticism of House of Orange
Battle of Aspern-Essling: Austrian Archduke Charles beatsNapoleonand his army. Napoleon's first defeat in 10 years.
1st bicycles (swift walkers) in US introduced in NYC
First US Democratic National Convention is held in Baltimore
Captain William Hobson proclaims British sovereignty over New Zealand; the North Island by treaty and the South Island by 'discovery'
1st steamship arrives in Hawaii
Lawrence, Kansas, is captured and sacked by pro-slavery forces
Richmond, Virginia, is designated the Confederate Capital
Siege of Port Hudson, Louisiana, begins
Battle of Spotsylvania Court House of the US Civil War ends inclusively with an estimated 32,000 casualties on both sides
General David Hunter takes command of Department of West Virginia
Russia declares an end to the Russo-Circassian War, forcing many Circassians into exile, commemorated today as the Circassian Day of Mourning
French Army attacks Paris, starting the Semaine Sanglante (Bloody Week), a weeklong battle that kills 10,000-15,000 and ends the Paris Commune
Battle of Iquique: Chilean naval forces overcome Peruvian ships (War of the Pacific)
US National Lawn Tennis Association is established in NYC, New York
Australian boxer Peter Jackson and future world heavyweight champion Jim Corbett fight a No Contest in 61 rounds at California Athletic Club, San Francisco
Ruggero Leoncavallo's opera "I Pagliacci" premieres at the Teatro Dal Verme in Milan
22-year-old French Anarchist Émile Henry is executed by guillotine. His last words were reputed to be "Courage, camarades! Vive l'anarchie!"
Yerkes Observatory 40" (1m) refractor used for 1st time
US Assay Office in Seattle, Washington, authorized
Federation Internationale de Football Association (FIFA) forms in Paris
France recalls its ambassador to the Vatican to protest the Pope's attempt to discipline two French bishops; this is yet another incident driving France and the Catholic Church apart
Louis H. Perlman patents a demountable tire carrying rim for cars
The US and Mexico sign an agreement over distribution of the waters of the Rio Grande, increasingly diverted to the US for irrigation
First American horror movie silent film "Dr. Jekyll and Mr. Hyde" premieres in Chicago
French troop enter Fez in Morocco to quell anti-European agitation
Greyhound Bus Company is founded by Carl Wickman in Hibbing, Minnesota
Britain begins "Summer Time" (daylight saving time)
Leo Pinckney, 1st American drafted during WWI
The Great Fire of Atlanta: at least 10,000 people were displaced, but there was only one fatality
US House of Representatives passes amendment allowing women to vote
Oldest radio station west of Mississippi River licensed in Greeley Co.
Colonel Jacob Ruppert buys out Colonel Tillinghast L’Hommedieu Huston's interest in NY Yankees for $1,500,000 to become sole owner
Rollin Kirby's "On the Road to Moscow" is the 1st cartoon to receive a Pulitzer Prize
Nathan Leopold & Richard Loeb kidnap and kill Bobby Franks to demonstrate their supposed intellectual superiority by committing a "perfect crime"
George Lloyd, Baron Lloyd of Dolobran, becomes British High Commissioner in Egypt
Norwegian explorer Roald Amundsun leaves Spitsbergen with 2 seaplanes for the North Pole
White Sox Earl Sheely hits a record 6th consecutive double
Automatic electric stock quotation board installed in NYC
Sergei Prokoviev's ballet "Prodigal Son" premieres in Paris
Oskaloosa, Iowa, becomes first US city to fingerprint its citizens
Sada Abe is arrested after wandering the streets of Tokyo for days with her dead lover's severed genitals in her hand. Her story soon becomes one of Japan's most notorious scandals.
AVRO chairman Willem Vogt fires all Jewish employees
Battle of Arras: British and French Allied forces counterattack south of Arras, France, winning time to reinforce and foritfy the Channel Ports
Paul Reynaud forms French government
German airforce occupies airport at Maleme, Crete
Singer Johan Heesters visits Dachau concentration camp
SS Robin Moor becomes the first US ship sunk by a U-boat during World War II
Convoy PQ16 departs Great Britain for Russia
Fastest 9 inning AL baseball night game (89 mins), Chicago White Sox beat visiting Washington Senators, 1-0

WWII: West Loch Disaster - explosion during munition loading kills at least 160 sailors, injures nearly 400, destroys six ships and damages 3 piers and several buildings at Pearl Harbor U.S. Naval Base in Oahu, Hawaii; details were kept classified until the early 1960s
Australian Services win 1st Victory Test Cricket at Lord's by 6 wickets

Physicist Louis Slotin is exposed to a lethal dose of radiation while preparing a plutonium core experiment at the Los Alamos lab, he dies 9 days later and the accident ends all hands-on nuclear assembly work at Los Alamos
Vietnamese troops of Ho Chi-Minh attack Cambodia

The opening of the Ninth Street Show, otherwise known as the 9th Street Art Exhibition - a gathering of a number of notable artists, and the stepping-out of the post war New York avant-garde, collectively know as the New York School.
Booklyn Dodgers score 15 runs in their 1st inning to beat Cincinnati Reds 19-1
French government of René Mayer resigns
US Twenty-sixth amendment to give 18-year-olds right to vote is defeated
1st transcontinental round-trip solo flight-sunrise to sunset
WFRV TV channel 5 in Green Bay, Wisconsin (ABC/NBC) begins broadcasting
Jordan government of Said el-Mufti forms
US explodes 1st airborne hydrogen bomb over Bikini Atoll
WITI TV channel 6 in Milwaukee, WI (CBS/ABC) begins broadcasting
Indonesian paratroopers reconquer Morotai Island
US performs nuclear test at Bikini Island (atmospheric tests)
Cleveland Metroparks Zoo Children's Petting Farm opens (Cleveland, Ohio)
Governor Patterson declares martial law in Montgomery, Alabama
Three more Cleveland HRs set the AL record for most HRs (26) over 8 games
1st nuclear-powered lighthouse begins operations (Chesapeake Bay)
Fire in Belgian resort kills 19
US begin intelligence flights above Laos
"Downtown" by Mrs Miller hits #82
A "loyalist" group calling itself the Ulster Volunteer Force (UVF) issued a statement declaring war on the Irish Republican Army (IRA)
Louie Louie by The Kingsmen reentered the chart & hits #97
Billy Williams of the Chicago Cubs sets outfielder record of 695 straight MLB games
Nuclear-powered sub USS Scorpion, with 99 men, reported missing & is later found at the bottom of the ocean off Azores
Paul McCartneyand girlfriend Jane Asher attend an Andy Williams concert
USSR performs nuclear test (underground)
WEKW TV channel 52 in Keene, NH (PBS) begins broadcasting
Rosariazo civil unrest occurs in Rosario, Argentina, following the death of a 15-year-old student
National Guard mobilizes to quell disturbances at Ohio State University
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
National Guard mobilizes to quell riot in Chattanooga, Tennessee
The Official Irish Republican Army (OIRA) kidnaps and shoots dead William Best (19), a soldier in the Royal Irish Rangers stationed in Germany whilst on leave at home
Lowell W. Perry is confirmed as chairman of the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission
Trial against Baader-Meinhof-group begins in Stuttgart
Electric Light Orchestra releases "Telephone Line" in the UK; it would peak in the Top 10 in the UK and US
Fire in hotel Duc de Brabant Brussels, kills 19
San Diego Padres beat Mont Expos, 11-8, in 21 innings
118 Unification church couples wed in England
Yamada Mumon Roshi appointed head of Zen Rinzai Sect
National Volksraad installed in Namibia
Stanley Cup Final, Montreal Forum, Montreal, Quebec: 4 consecutive titles for Montreal Canadiens; beat NY Rangers, 4-1 for a 4 games to 1 series win
US Coast Guard Ensign Jean Marie Butler is 1st woman to graduate from US service academy
Stanley Cup Final, Nassau Coliseum, Uniondale, NY: NY Islanders win back-to-back titles; beat Minnesota North Stars, 5-1 for 4 games to 1 series win
"WKRP in Cincinnati", an ensemble TV sitcom created by Hugh Wilson and set in a struggling radio station, 90th and final episode airs on CBS
British troops land on Falkland Islands, in the South Atlantic, to repel Argentine military invasion
"Bang The Drum All Day" single by Todd Rundgren hits #63
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida), for mating for STS-7 mission
Israel exchanges 1,150 prisoners with the PFLP-GC in return for 3 Israeli soldiers
Atlanta Brave Rafael Ramirez hits 4 doubles in a game
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Xignals PC Board BBS begins in Alabama
"Da'Butt" by EU hits #35
New building for the National Gallery of Canada, designed by Moshe Safdie, opens in Ottawa, Ontario
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Dow Jones Industrial Average hits a record 2,844.68
Ethiopia's Marxist president (Mengistu Haile Mariam) resigns
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
New Jersey Senate overrides Governor Florio's veto & lowers sales tax to 6%
Reality TV series The Real World, created by Mary-Ellis Bunim and Jonathan Murray and considered one of the first modern reality shows, debuts on MTV
Opposition and independence leader Xanana Gusmao of East Timor sentenced to life in prison (released 1999)
Robin Smith scores 167 in England Texaco Trophy loss v Australia
Venezuela president Carlos Andres Perez fired
MLB Cincinnati Reds bat out of order against Los Angeles Dodgers in 2nd inning
South Yemen secedes from Yemen
Blackout in many areas of Queens, NY
Ken Griffey Jr, 26, is 8th youngest to hits 200 home runs
The Trappist Martyrs of Atlas are executed.
Blue Jays pitcherRoger Clemensbeats the Yankees for his 200th win
Five abortion clinics in Miami, Florida, are targeted by a butyric acid attack
French Taubira law officially recognizes the Atlantic slave trade and slavery as crimes against humanity.
The Enron Corporation's power generating venture in India, the Dabhol Power Company, serves formal notice that it will terminate its power supply contract and pull out
Earthquake hits northern Algeria, killing more than 2,000 people
Stanislav Petrov receives the World Citizen Award for averting a potential nuclear war in 1983, after he correctly identified a fault in the Soviet early warning system
In a referendum, 55% of Montenegrins vote for independence from the State Union of Serbia and Montenegro
Swedish ice hockey team Tre Kronor takes gold in the World Championship, first nation to hold both the World and Olympic titles in the same year
Cutty Sark, the last surviving tea clipper, is badly damaged by fire in Greenwich, England.
120 people are killed and 350 injured by a suicide bomb in Sana'a, Yemen
13 people are killed and 22 people injured after a bus falls 80 metres off a cliff in Albania
Microsoft announces the release of Xbox One
José Mário Vaz is elected President of Guinea-Bissau
Thai army declares martial law and closes down several news stations
Flavor Flavis arrested near Las Vegas on charges including speeding and driving under the influence
Mullah Akhtar Muhammad Mansour, leader of the Afghan Taliban is reportedly killed by a US drone in Pakistan

On same card, American boxer Jermell Charlo KOs John Jackson in 8th to claim vacant WBC super welterweight title, and Jermall Charlo beats Austin Trout on points to retain IBF version; first twins to hold world championships in same weight division
The Tradition Senior Men's Golf, Greystone G&CC: Defending champion Berhard Langer wins by 5 strokes from Scott Parel & Scott McCarron
Mushrooms have poisoned more than 800 in western Iran, killing 11
Teenager who started Oregon's 2017 Eagle Creek Fire while igniting fireworks is ordered to pay $36.6 million to cover damages by district judge
Truckers begin a 10 strike in Brazil, blocking roads in protest at the price of diesel leading to country-wide shortages
Austria's far-right Freedom Party resigns from the ruling coalition after two of their ministers are sacked for offering government contracts for political influence in a video
French crown prince Louis, invades England with 700 ships, having been invited by English barons at war withKing John
Hao Jing, envoy of Mongol leaderKublai Khanimprisoned by order of the high Chancellor of China, Jia Sidao at the Song Dynasty court of Emperor Lizong while attempting to negotiate with the Song
Treaty of Troyes:Henry Vof England and his heirs will inherit the throne of France upon the death of King Charles VI of France (later rendered moot by the military victory of Charles VII)
KingEdward IVenters London in triumph after victory at the Battle of Teekesbury with Queen Margaret as captive
Francisco De Bobadilla appointed Governor of the Indies, succeedingChristopher Columbus
Reknown French preacherJacques-Bénigne Bossuetselected as Bishop of Meaux
Order of St.Alexander Nevskyis instituted in Russia by Empress Catherine I. Discontinued and then reinstated by the Soviet government in 1942 as the Order of Alexander Nevsky.
Napoleonand his forces abandon their siege of Acre after two months - turning point in French invasion of Egypt and Syria
Frederic Mistral, Joseph Roumanille, and five other Provencal poets found Félibrige, a literary and cultural association
American Red Cross founded byClara Barton
Louis Bothabecomes the first Prime Minister of the Union of South Africa
Mexican PresidentVenustiano Carranzais executed by army generals after fleeing an armed rebellion in Mexico
Pulitzer prize awarded toEugene O'Neillfor his play "Anna Christie"
AviatorCharles Lindbergh, in the Spirit of St. Louis, lands in Paris after the first solo air crossing of the Atlantic
Yankees' legendary sluggerBabe Ruthhits 3 HRs as NY blows 6-0 lead in 4th against Philadelphia A's at Shibe Park; go on to lose, 15-7
Mount Davidson Cross, San Francisco, lit byFDRvia telegraph
Nazi SS-ReichsfuehrerHeinrich Himmlercaptured
NY YankJoe Dimaggiohits for cycle (single, double, triple, HR)
DutchQueen Julianaopens Amsterdam-Rhine Canal
French government ofGuy Molletresigns
Leontyne Pricebecomes the first African American to sing the lead at Teatro alla Scala in Milan in "Aida"
Muhammad AliTKOs Henry Cooper in 6 for heavyweight boxing title
Robert F. Kennedy's murdererSirhan Sirhansentenced to death, later commuted to life imprisonment
Mel Stottlemyreties MLB record by walking 11, but wins 2-0 over the Washington Senators at Yankee Stadium
Marvin Gaye' eleventh album "What's Going On" is released; considered the best album off all-time by critic surveys in Rolling Stone (2020); The Guardian (1997); and New Music Express (1985)
Michelangelo's Pietà in St. Peter's Basilica in Rome is damaged by a vandal
Stevie Wonder's single "Sir Duke", a tribute toDuke Ellingtonand other musical influences of Wonder's, begins a 3-week run at #1
"Star WarsEpisode V - Empire Strikes Back", produced byGeorge Lucasopens in cinemas in UK and North America
François Mitterrandbecomes president of France
Reggae musicianBob Marleyreceives a Jamaican state funeral
David Bowie's "Let's Dance" single goes #1
Military coup in Fiji under Colonel SitivaniRabuka
43rd Cannes Film Festival: "Wild at Heart" directed byDavid LynchstarringNicolas CageandLaura Dernwins the Palme d'Or
Last episode of "Newhart" airs on CBS-TV, with a special twist of an ending
"You're Makin' Me High" single released byToni Braxton(Grammy Award - Best Female R&B Vocal Performance, 1997)
Indonesian presidentSuhartoresigns after 31 years in power
All My Children starSusan Luccifinally wins a Daytime Emmy after being nominated 19 times, the longest period of unsuccessful nominations in television history
American singer-songwriterCarole Kingreceives Library of Congress Gershwin Prize at the White House in Washington, D.C.
RussianPresident Putinsigns agreements with China in Beijing in relation to trade and infrastructure
Barnum & BaileyCircus performs for the last time at the Nassau Coliseum in NYC after 146 years
BTSwins the Top Social Artist at the Billboard Awards, becoming the first K-pop group to win any Billboard Award
Former US PresidentBarack Obamaand MichelleObamasign a production deal with Netflix to produce series and movies for the streaming service
US Justice Department says it is expanding its internal investigation into whether FBI infiltratedDonald Trump's 2016 campaign
Indonesian PresidentJoko Widodoconfirmed as winning re-election, defeating Prabowo Subianto with 55.5% of the vote
After flying for 17 hours from Newfoundland,Amelia Earhartlands near Londonderry, Northern Ireland, completing the first solo transatlantic flight by a woman

Dan White convicted of the voluntary manslaughter of San Francisco mayor George Moscone and openly gay councilorHarvey Milk. The conviction on a lesser charge outraged the gay community and led to the White Night riots.
Shinran, Japanese Buddhist monk and founder of what became the Jōdo Shinshū sect of Japanese Buddhism, born in Heian Kyō, Yamashiro Province (d. 1263)
